results.student_answer: The Storm Prediction Center (SPC) began monitoring the imminent threat that included this tornado event on May 3, 2020, at 12:17 p.m. CDT (UTC-05:00), when they issued a moderate risk of severe weather for parts of Louisiana and Mississippi. This risk assessment indicated a 30% chance of strong or violent tornadoes within the specified areas. The tornado that caused significant damage in Kentwood, Louisiana, and other areas was one of the tornadoes that fell within this moderate risk area.

results.question: When did the Kentwood–Carson tornado occur and what were its start and end times?
results.reference_answer: Saturday, March 15, 2025; it began at 12:17 p.m. CDT and dissipated at 1:39 p.m. CDT.
results.student_answer: The Kentwood–Carson tornado occurred on May 22, 2020. Its start time was at 12:17 p.m. CDT (UTC-05:00), and its end time was at approximately 12:45 p.m. CDT (UTC-05:00).

results.question: Where did the Kentwood–Carson tornado begin?
results.reference_answer: One mile east-northeast of Kentwood in Tangipahoa Parish, Louisiana.
results.student_answer: The Kentwood–Carson tornado began at 12:17 p.m. CDT (UTC-05:00), just off of LA 38 and to the east of I-55, one mile to the east-northeast of Kentwood in Tangipahoa Parish, Louisiana.

results.question: How many people did the EF4 Kentwood–Carson tornado kill?
results.student_answer: The EF4 Kentwood–Carson tornado killed two people.

results.question: What maximum wind speed and Enhanced Fujita rating did the National Weather Service assign to the tornado?
results.reference_answer: Maximum winds of 170 mph (270 km/h), rated a low-end EF4.
results.student_answer: The National Weather Service assigned a maximum wind speed of 170 mph (274 km/h) and an Enhanced Fujita (EF) rating of EF3 to the tornado.

results.question: What damage did the tornado cause to the Paradise Ranch RV resort in Walthall County?
results.reference_answer: It destroyed all 27 cabins and 210 campsites.
results.student_answer: The tornado caused significant damage to the Paradise Ranch RV resort in Walthall County, including damaging or destroying several RVs and cabins. The extent of the damage was not specified in the provided text, but it was mentioned that the resort sustained damage.
